---
title: "2.5 Compound Boolean Expressions"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Combine conditions to make richer decisions

A <span class="term">compound Boolean expression</span> (复合布尔表达式) combines smaller Boolean expressions using:

::: {.tight-list}
- `&&` for <span class="term">and</span>
- `||` for <span class="term">or</span>
- `!` for <span class="term">not</span>
:::

```java
if (age >= 13 && age <= 19)
{
    System.out.println("teenager");
}
```

## And

### `&&` is true only when both sides are true

```java
int age = 15;
boolean hasTicket = true;

if (age >= 13 && hasTicket)
{
    System.out.println("Enter the event");
}
```

Truth rule:

| A | B | `A && B` |
|---|---|---|
| true | true | true |
| true | false | false |
| false | true | false |
| false | false | false |

## Or

### `||` is true when at least one side is true

```java
boolean isWeekend = false;
boolean isHoliday = true;

if (isWeekend || isHoliday)
{
    System.out.println("No school");
}
```

Truth rule:

| A | B | `A || B` |
|---|---|---|
| true | true | true |
| true | false | true |
| false | true | true |
| false | false | false |

## Number Range

### `&&` is the standard inside-range pattern

```java
int x = 7;
if (x >= 1 && x <= 10)
{
    System.out.println("inside range");
}
```

For outside the range, use `||`:

```java
if (x < 1 || x > 10)
{
    System.out.println("outside range");
}
```

## Not

### `!` flips a Boolean value

```java
boolean finished = false;

if (!finished)
{
    System.out.println("Keep working");
}
```

`!` is useful, but it can make expressions harder to read. Prefer a direct condition when the direct form is clearer.

## Truth Table Checkpoint

### Fill in the result column

| `x` | `y` | Expression | Result |
|---:|---:|---|---|
| 4 | 9 | `x < 5 && y > 5` | `true` |
| 4 | 9 | `x < 5 || y < 5` | `true` |
| 4 | 9 | `!(x < 5)` | `false` |
| 4 | 9 | `x >= 5 && y >= 5` | `false` |

Class move: evaluate the smaller comparisons first, then combine the Boolean results.

## Quick Check

### Trace compound booleans

What is printed?

```java
int a = 6;
int b = 10;
if (a % 2 == 0 && b > 8)
{
    System.out.println("A");
}
if (a > 6 || b == 10)
{
    System.out.println("B");
}
```

Answer:

```text
A
B
```

## Short Circuit Evaluation

### Java may skip the second condition

For `&&`, if the first condition is false, the whole expression is false.

```java
if (x != 0 && 10 / x > 1)
{
    System.out.println("safe");
}
```

For `||`, if the first condition is true, the whole expression is true.

Short circuiting prevents unnecessary or unsafe checks.

## Coding Challenge

### Truth Tables POGIL

Write an `if` statement that prints `true` only when:

::: {.tight-list}
- `x` is between `1` and `10`, inclusive
- `y` is not equal to `0`
:::

```java
int x = 5;
int y = 2;

if (x >= 1 && x <= 10 && y != 0)
{
    System.out.println("true");
}
```

Test at least one boundary value such as `x = 1` or `x = 10`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain the truth rules for `&&`, `||`, and `!`
- use `&&` for an inside range and `||` for an outside range
- evaluate compound Boolean expressions step by step
- explain short circuit evaluation for `&&` and `||`
- write readable compound conditions with correct operator placement
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
